title: Isolation of Single Nuclei from Human Brain and Immunostaining for NeuN
author: Marcos Otero-Garcia
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6tuhenw

steps.title: Prepare Homogenization Buffer and cool it on ice
steps.instruction: Prepare Homogenization Buffer and cool it on ice. %s %s

steps.title: Pre-cool the dounce tissue grinder on...
steps.instruction: Pre-cool the dounce tissue grinder on ice ( Kimble Kontes all glass tissue grinder , 7 mL tubes, 0.02 - 0.056 mm clearance space between pestle and tubes)

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.4 mL of Homogenization buffer to the dounce tissue grinder.

steps.title: Collect the brain chunk (~ 100...
steps.instruction: Collect the brain chunk (~ 100 mg ) and transfer to a Petri dish on ice.

steps.title: Cut out into small pieces using...
steps.instruction: Cut out into small pieces using a chilled scalpel or blade.

steps.title: Transfer all pieces into the dounce tissue grinder
steps.instruction: Transfer all pieces into the dounce tissue grinder.

steps.title: Homogenize the tissue, on ice ( ~30 firm strokes )
steps.instruction: Homogenize the tissue, on ice ( ~30 firm strokes ). Check on hematocytometer while homogenizing and adjust the number of strokes. Too few strokes – you will see cell clumps; too many strokes – you will get damaged nuclei.

steps.title: Filter homogenate using 40 µm Corning...
steps.instruction: Filter homogenate using 40 µm Corning cell strainer to remove clumps. --> Take sample for hemocytometer.

steps.title: Transfer the homogenate into two precooled 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the homogenate into two precooled 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:08:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:08:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Slowly aspirate the supernatant from the side of the tube
steps.instruction: Slowly aspirate the supernatant from the side of the tube. Avoid disturbing the pellets, up to 50 µL of supernatant can be left.

steps.title: Gently resuspend each pellet in 225...
steps.instruction: Gently resuspend each pellet in 225 µL (final volume) of cold Homogenization buffer and pool both tubes (final volume 450 µL ).

steps.title: Add an equal volume of cold 50 %v/v, iodixanol
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of cold 50 %v/v, iodixanol . Critical! Be very exact with volumes. 450 µL suspension + 450 µL 50 % iod. Final iodixanol concentration is 25 % .

steps.title: Add an equal volume of 29...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of 29 % iodixanol ( 900 µL ) into a 2 ml Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Slowly layer off the 25 %...
steps.instruction: Slowly layer off the 25 % iodixanol suspension mix over the 25 % iodixanol, without mixing them.

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:20:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:20:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Prepare buffer for immunostaining
steps.instruction: Prepare buffer for immunostaining. %s %s

steps.title: After centrifugation, remove and discard the...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, remove and discard the top myelin-rich debris layer (you can use a 1 ml pipette with the tip cut, or cotton swabs).

steps.title: Then, remove and discard the aqueous...
steps.instruction: Then, remove and discard the aqueous supernatant, without disrupting the nuclei pellet . Avoid contaminating with the top layer.

steps.title: Use a small amount of buffer...
steps.instruction: Use a small amount of buffer for Antibody Incubation to resuspend the pellet and transfer the solution to a new tube.

steps.title: Gently resuspend in 500 µL of buffer for Antibody Incubation
steps.instruction: Gently resuspend in 500 µL of buffer for Antibody Incubation .

steps.title: Incubate for 00:15:00 , at 4...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:15:00 , at 4 °C or iced, for blocking nonspecific staining.

steps.title: Take sample for hemocytometer
steps.instruction: Take sample for hemocytometer.

steps.title: Take a sample for unstained control
steps.instruction: Take a sample for unstained control. Take sample for 2AB-only (nonspecific binding control).

steps.title: Add primary antibody ( Ms-a-NeuN, 1:1000...
steps.instruction: Add primary antibody ( Ms-a-NeuN, 1:1000 ) and incubate on a rotator in cold room ( 4 °C ) for 00:40:00 . Anti-NeuN Antibody, clone A60 Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #MAB377 Use Eppendorfs coated with BSA for collection (to coat the Eppendorf tubes, fill them with 10 % BSA solution in PBS for 00:05:00 , rinse with PBS, and dry at 4 °C overnight).

steps.title: Add 700 µL of buffer for...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL of buffer for Immunostaining and invert several times.

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Carefully transfer all the supernatant (don’t leave any supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer all the supernatant (don’t leave any supernatant! It’s ok to drag some pellet) to a new Eppendorf tube and resuspend the pellet in 400 µL of buffer for Antibody Incubation .

steps.title: Centrifuge the supernatant at for 00:05:00...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the supernatant at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C to recover non-pelleted nuclei.

steps.title: Resuspend them in 200 µL of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend them in 200 µL of buffer for Antibody Incubation and pool for a final volume of 600 µl.

steps.title: Take samples for single color staining
steps.instruction: Take samples for single color staining.

steps.title: Add secondary antibody ( G-a-Ms Alexa...
steps.instruction: Add secondary antibody ( G-a-Ms Alexa Fluor 647, 1:1000 ) and Hoechst 33258 (5 µg/ml) to the same tube. ( Hoechst 1:1000 of 2,5 mg/ml stock ).

steps.title: Check the quality of the sample and on hemocytometer
steps.instruction: Check the quality of the sample and on hemocytometer. For 100 mg of tissue, should have ~ 2 Million nuclei in ~0.6 ml.

steps.title: Transfer to 7 ml culture tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer to 7 ml culture tubes.

steps.title: Place on ice and bring them...
steps.instruction: Place on ice and bring them to the FACS facility for sorting. FACS sorting controls: Non-stained 2AB only Single stainings

steps.title: Collect in BSA coated Eppendorfs containing...
steps.instruction: Collect in BSA coated Eppendorfs containing collection medium (containing ~1/5 of the expected final volume after collection).

steps.title: Add BSA after collection for final...
steps.instruction: Add BSA after collection for final 1% BSA final concentration (tested for 10x Chromium v2 and v3). Different Single Cell assays tolerate different BSA concentrations, but lowering it may increase nuclei aggregation.

steps.title: Add 2925 µL, IM1
steps.instruction: Add 2925 µL, IM1 .

steps.title: Add 3 µL, DTT 1mM
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL, DTT 1mM .

steps.title: Add 30 µL, 50x Protease Inhibitor
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL, 50x Protease Inhibitor .

steps.title: Add 15 µL, RNaseIN 40U/µl
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL, RNaseIN 40U/µl .

steps.title: Add 30 µL, TritonX100 10 %v/v
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL, TritonX100 10 %v/v .

steps.title: Add 9400 µL, RNase-free PBS (pH7
steps.instruction: Add 9400 µL, RNase-free PBS (pH7.4) .

steps.title: Add 500 µL, RNase-free 10 % BSA
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL, RNase-free 10 % BSA .

steps.title: Add 50 µL, 1 M MgCl2
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL, 1 M MgCl2 .

steps.title: Add 10 µL, DNAse I (2000 U/ml)
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL, DNAse I (2000 U/ml) .

steps.title: Prepare buffer for Antibody incubation (1
steps.instruction: Prepare buffer for Antibody incubation (1.5 ml per sample) by adding 5 µL of RNaseIN 40 U/µl to 1 mL of buffer for Immunostaining (final concentration 0.2U/µl)
